en.extract:
The chestnut-winged babbler (Cyanoderma erythropterum) is a babbler species in the family Timaliidae. 


Description
The chestnut-winged babbler is chestnut-brown with a greyish face and underparts, and is 12.5–13.5 cm (4.9–5.3 in) long. It feeds on small Coleoptera beetles, Phasmida insects, ants, and Hemiptera bugs. Its foraging strategy is gleaning.
Timalia erythroptera was the scientific name proposed by Edward Blyth in 1842 for an olive-brown babbler from Nepal.
It was later placed in the genus Stachyris, but since 2020 is recognised as a Cyanoderma species. The grey-hooded babbler (C. bicolor) of Borneo was formerly considered conspecific.


Distribution and habitat
The chestnut-winged babbler  occurs in the Malay Peninsula from southern Thailand to Singapore, and in Sumatra.  It inhabits forests and shrublands up to an elevation of 800 m (2,600 ft). It is listed as Least Concern on the IUCN Red List.


References


External links
BirdLife International (2019). "Chestnut-winged Babbler Cyanoderma erythropterum".
"Photos of Chestnut-winged Babbler Cyanoderma erythropterum". The Internet Bird Collection.

The '''chestnut-winged babbler''' ('''''Cyanoderma erythropterum''''') is a babbler species in the family [[Timaliidae]]. 

== Description ==
The chestnut-winged babbler is chestnut-brown with a greyish face and underparts, and is {{cvt|12.5-13.5|cm}} long. It feeds on small [[Coleoptera]] beetles, [[Phasmida]] insects, [[ant]]s, and [[Hemiptera]] bugs.<ref name=HBoW2016>{{cite book |year=2021 |editor=del Hoyo, J. |editor2=Elliott, A. |editor3=Sargatal, J. |editor4=Christie, D. A. |editor5=de Juana, E. |title=Handbook of the Birds of the World |publisher=Lynx Edicions |location=Barcelona |volume=((2: Passerines)) |last1=Collar |first1=N. J. |last2=Robson |first2=C. |chapter=Chestnut-winged Babbler (''Cyanoderma erythroptera'') |doi=10.2173/bow.chwbab1.02 |chapter-url=https://www.hbw.com/species/chestnut-winged-babbler-cyanoderma-erythropterum}}</ref> Its foraging strategy is [[Gleaning (birds)|gleaning]].<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Juliana |first=J. |last2=Gawin |first2=D. F. |date=2020 |title=Foraging behaviour of three sympatric babblers (Family: Timaliidae) |journal=Trends in Undergraduate Research |volume=3 |issue=2 |pages=a26–34 |doi=10.33736/tur.2138.2020|doi-access=free }}</ref>

''Timalia erythroptera'' was the [[scientific name]] proposed by [[Edward Blyth]] in 1842 for an olive-brown babbler from Nepal.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Blyth |first1=E. |year=1842 |title=Report from the Curator |journal=The Journal of the Asiatic Society of Bengal |volume=11 |issue=128 |pages=788–799 |url=https://archive.org/details/journalofasiatic112asia/page/788}}</ref>
It was later placed in the genus ''[[Stachyris]]'', but since 2020 is recognised as a ''[[Cyanoderma]]'' species.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Moyle |first1=R. G. |last2=Andersen |first2=M. J. |last3=Oliveros |first3=C. H. |last4=Steinheimer |first4=F. D. |last5=Reddy |first5=S. |year=2012 |title=Phylogeny and Biogeography of the Core Babblers (Aves: Timaliidae) |journal=Systematic Biology |volume=61 |issue=4 |pages=631–651 |doi=10.1093/sysbio/sys027 |pmid=22328569 |doi-access=free}}</ref><ref name=HBoW2016/> The [[grey-hooded babbler]] (''C. bicolor'') of Borneo was formerly considered conspecific.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Species Updates – IOC World Bird List|url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/updates/species-updates/|access-date=2021-05-28}}</ref>

== Distribution and habitat ==
The chestnut-winged babbler  occurs in the [[Malay Peninsula]] from southern [[Thailand]] to [[Singapore]], and in [[Sumatra]].  It inhabits forests and shrublands up to an elevation of {{cvt|800|m}}. It is listed as [[Least Concern]] on the [[IUCN Red List]].<ref name="iucn" />

zh.extract:
红翅穗鹛（学名：Stachyris erythroptera），是画眉科穗鹛属的一种，分布于文莱、缅甸、越南、泰国、印度尼西亚、马来西亚和新加坡。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
红翅穗鹛的平均体重约为12.6克。栖息地包括种植园、亚热带或热带的湿润低地林、亚热带或热带的（低地）湿润疏灌丛、亚热带或热带的沼泽林和亚热带或热带的红树林。


参考文献
Stachyris erythroptera. BirdLife.org.   [2011-06-27]. （原始内容存档于2016-03-05）.
